request.question: What is the population of the municipality which is located about 230 km Southwest of Dorgali?

title: Elvas
Dorgali
Tartarugalzinho
Abu Simbel temples
Galliavola
Verkhneuralsk
Holskardvatnet
Cagliari
Bransfield Basin
Bikin
sentences: Elvas (] ) is a Portuguese municipality, former episcopal city and frontier fortress of easternmost central Portugal, located in the district of Portalegre in Alentejo.
It is situated about 230 km east of Lisbon, and about 15 km west of the Spanish fortress of Badajoz, by the Madrid-Badajoz-Lisbon railway.
The municipality population as of 2011 was 23,078, in an area of 631.29 km2 .
The city itself had a population of 16,640 as of 2011 .

Dorgali (Sardinian: "Durgali" ) is a "comune" (municipality) in the Province of Nuoro in the Italian region Sardinia, located about 230 km northeast of Cagliari and about 38 km east of Nuoro in the Seaside Supramonte mountain area.
Tartarugalzinho ("Big Tartarugal") is a municipality located in the east of the state of Amapá in Brazil.
Its population is 12,563 and its area is 6,712 km2 .
Tartarugalzinho is located 230 km from the state capitol of Macapá.
It was established as a municipality on December 17, 1987.
The Abu Simbel temples are two massive rock temples at Abu Simbel (أبو سمبل in Arabic), a village in Nubia, southern Egypt, near the border with Sudan.
They are situated on the western bank of Lake Nasser, about 230 km southwest of Aswan (about 300 km by road).
The complex is part of the UNESCO World Heritage Site known as the "Nubian Monuments", which run from Abu Simbel downriver to Philae (near Aswan).

The twin temples were originally carved out of the mountainside during the 19th dynasty reign of the Pharaoh Ramesses II in the 13th century BC, as a lasting monument to himself and his queen Nefertari, to commemorate his victory at the Battle of Kadesh.
Their huge external rock relief figures have become iconic.
Galliavola is a "comune" (municipality) in the Province of Pavia in the Italian region Lombardy, located about 50 km southwest of Milan and about 30 km southwest of Pavia.
As of 31 December 2004, it had a population of 230 and an area of 8.5 km².

Verkhneuralsk (Russian: Верхнеура́льск ) is a town and the administrative center of Verkhneuralsky District in Chelyabinsk Oblast, Russia, located in the upper streams of the Ural River, 230 km southwest of Chelyabinsk, the administrative center of the oblast.
Population:  (2010 Census) ;  (2002 Census) ;  (1989 Census)
Holskardvatnet is a lake in the municipality Vik in Sogn og Fjordane county, Norway.
It is located just less than 100 m north of the municipality (and county) border with Modalen in Hordaland county.

The lake is 20 km south of the village of Ortnevik in neighboring Høyanger Municipality, about 13 km southwest of the village of Arnafjord, and about 28 km southwest of the municipal center of Vikøyri.
The 4.81 km2 lake is located at an elevation of 845 m above sea level.
Cagliari ( , or ; ] ; Sardinian: "Casteddu" ; ) is an Italian municipality and the capital of the island of Sardinia, an autonomous region of Italy.
Cagliari's Sardinian name "Casteddu" literally means "castle".
It has about 150,000 inhabitants, while its metropolitan city (including Cagliari and 16 other nearby municipalities) has more than 431,000 inhabitants.
According to Eurostat, the population of the Functional urban area, the commuting zone of Cagliari, rises to 476,974.
The Bransfield Basin is a back-arc rift basin located off the Northern tip of the Antarctic Peninsula.
The basin lies within a Northeast and Southwest trending strait that separates the peninsula from the nearby South Shetland Islands to the Northwest.
The basin extends for more than 500 km from Smith Island (South Shetland Islands) to a portion of the Hero Fracture Zone.
The basin can be subdivided into three basins: Western, Central, and Eastern.

The Western basin is 130 km long by 70 km wide with a depth of 1.3 km , the Central basin is 230 km long by 60 km wide with a depth of 1.9 km , and the Eastern basin is 150 km long by 40 km wide with a depth of over 2.7 km .
The three basins are separated by the Deception Island and Bridgeman Island.
The moho depth in the region has been seismically interpreted to be roughly 34 km deep.
Bikin (Russian: Бики́н ) is a town in Khabarovsk Krai, Russia, located on the Bikin River (a tributary of the Ussuri) 230 km southwest of Khabarovsk.
Population:  (2010 Census) ;  (2002 Census) ;  (1989 Census) ; 19,000 (1967).

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Produce a single, concise, structured first-hop evidence report as the `summary`. The summary must do all of the following and only include content that helps determine what to fetch next:

- Enumerate concrete facts found in the provided passages. For each fact include the passage source label and either a very short quoted fragment (up to about 15 words) when a decisive short fact appears or a brief paraphrase otherwise. Keep each fact to one short line.

- Explicitly identify the bridge entity(ies) and any missing or ambiguous fact(s) that prevent answering the question from the given passages. If a name or identifier in the question corresponds to multiple candidate pages present in `passages`, list every candidate variant and mark the name as ambiguous; do not choose between them.

- Prescribe concrete, prioritized next-hop retrieval targets to resolve each missing or ambiguous element. For each target, specify which page(s) or disambiguation page to fetch and what exact section, keyword, or datum to extract (for example: specific field such as birth year, office held, list membership, dates, or the section heading to check). Prioritize targets (1, 2, 3...) when helpful.

- Only produce a final answer in `summary` if every required fact for every entity is present and unambiguous in the supplied passages; otherwise do not produce a final answer and focus on retrieval guidance.

Formatting and content constraints:
- Keep the summary concise (a short paragraph or a few short bullet-like lines).
- Produce only the single output field `summary` and nothing else.
- Preserve source-linked evidence; do not assert facts without a passage label.
- Do not invent facts or assume which ambiguous candidate is intended.
- Avoid copying long verbatim spans; use short quoted fragments when decisive and paraphrase otherwise.
- When an expected authoritative page is missing from `passages`, note that absence and include that page title as a next-hop retrieval target.

Input: fields `question` and `passages`. Output: one field `summary` meeting the requirements above.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

You are to produce exactly one compact second-hop retrieval query that targets the missing bridge or supporting evidence referenced by summary_1. Follow these operational rules when constructing the single `query` string:

- Use visible anchor tokens from `question` or `summary_1` (named works, titles, artist names, years or other identifiers only if present) and append a short retrieval cue naming the bridge sought (e.g., artist, album, recording, genre, season count, passage, supporting quote).
- Emit one concise line (one string assigned to `query`), roughly ≤ 10–12 words, formatted either as a short interrogative that names the anchors or as a compact keyword string (anchors + bridge keywords).
- If summary_1 explicitly requests a missing attribute or passage, emit the focused retrieval query rather than asking for clarification.
- Do not include answers, conclusions, enumerations, provenance notes, speculative language, or user-facing explanations; do not invent entities or add tokens that are not present in the inputs.
- Include year or other identifier tokens only if they appear in the provided inputs and help disambiguate.
- Keep wording retrieval-oriented and terse. Preserve the fallback that if retrieval using this query later fails, downstream steps may request clarification.

Output contract: produce exactly one field `query` whose value is the single compact retrieval query line described above.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Instructions for producing `summary` (single field only):
1. Merge the first-hop `context` and the second-hop `passages` into one compact integrated `summary` that preserves the primary answer name(s) and only includes passage-supported facts needed to justify or disambiguate that answer.
2. If `context` already contains a concise answer (a short sentence or a name), keep that content as the first sentence of `summary` when it is consistent with facts in `passages`. If `context` lacks a concise answer, create a concise first sentence that names the primary answer(s), using the canonical labels from `context` when available and otherwise from `passages`.
3. Expand the summary only when `passages` provide explicit, matching second-hop evidence about one or more named entities referenced in `context`. When expansion is appropriate, append at most one short supporting sentence per named entity. Each supporting sentence must begin with the entity's full name as shown in the inputs and paraphrase up to a few core attributes that are directly present in the `passages` (examples: nationality, a notable award/status, a current affiliation/team, a birth date, membership/partnership relation, or an explicit founding/formation fact).
4. Keep `summary` concise: typically 1–2 sentences for a single entity; allow up to 3 sentences only when multiple distinct named entities each require one supporting sentence. Prioritize the facts most directly necessary to answer the `question`.
5. Only assert facts that are directly supported by the provided `passages` or `context`. Do not introduce new entities, dates, awards, affiliations, roles, or causal claims that are not present in the inputs.
6. Prefer paraphrase over long verbatim copying. Use short quoted phrases only when the exact short phrase (roughly ≤10 words) appears in the `passages` and quoting preserves clarity.
7. If `context` and `passages` conflict, prefer facts that are explicitly present in the `passages`: update the concise answer to match passage-supported content rather than preserving a contradicted `context`.
8. If the retrieved `passages` do not supply explicit supporting evidence for named entities in `context`, keep the `summary` concise and do not expand or speculate.
9. Output exactly one field named `summary` whose value is the integrated post-retrieval summary text. Do not output additional fields, metadata, or commentary.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Follow these operational rules when producing `answer`:
- Use only the content of `question`, `summary_1`, and `summary_2` as your evidential basis. Do not introduce new facts or external information.
- If both `summary_1` and `summary_2` clearly and unambiguously support the same binary (yes/no) conclusion to the question, set `answer` to exactly one lowercase token: yes or no. The `answer` field must contain nothing else (no punctuation, capitalization, quotes, explanation, or additional text).
- Decide which token to emit by aggregating the visible summaries only. Treat `summary_2` as the later integrated evidence; do not let unresolved language in `summary_1` alone prevent emitting the single-token result if both summaries now unambiguously agree.
- If the summaries conflict, are ambiguous, incomplete, hedged such that the relation is unresolved, or if the question is non-binary, do not apply the single-token rule; instead follow the agent's existing final-answer fallback behavior for unresolved or non-binary cases (which may produce a fuller response or an uncertainty signal).

Produce only the `answer` field as specified.

Seimsdalen | Seimsdalen is a valley in the municipality of Årdal in Sogn og Fjordane county, Norway.  The valley is located 3 km west of the municipal center of Årdalstangen, to which it is connected via a 1.5 km long .  The village of Indre Offerdal lies about 10 km to the southwest.  The population of the valley is 467 (2013).

The municipality population as of 2011 was 23,078, in an area of 631.29 km2 .  The city itself had a population of 16,640 as of 2011 .

response.summary_1:
- [1] Dorgali — quoted: "located about 230 km northeast of Cagliari" (implies Cagliari is ~230 km southwest of Dorgali).  
- No passage provided for "Cagliari" (municipality); population not present in supplied passages.

Bridge entity and missing/ambiguous facts:
- Bridge entity: Cagliari (the municipality implied by [1]). Missing fact: "population of Cagliari" (and the reference year) is not in the passages.

Next-hop retrieval targets (prioritized):
1. Fetch page "Cagliari" (municipality/comune) — extract infobox fields "Population total" and "Population as of [year]" (or the lead sentence population figure and its date).  
2. If "Cagliari" has multiple candidate pages, fetch "Cagliari (disambiguation)" and then the main "Cagliari (comune)" page; extract same population fields.  
3. Optionally re-fetch "Dorgali" page's lead to confirm the exact phrasing/location if needed (section: lead/infobox).  

Do not answer population here because the required population datum for the bridge entity (Cagliari) is absent from the supplied passages.
response.hop2_query: Cagliari population total and "population as of" (infobox)
hop2_docs: Metropolitan City of Cagliari | The Metropolitan City of Cagliari (Italian: "Città metropolitana di Cagliari" ) is a metropolitan city in Sardinia, Italy.  Its capital is the city of Cagliari and includes 17 comuni.  It was established by law in 2016 and replaced the Province of Cagliari.  The current president is the mayor of Cagliari, Massimo Zedda.  The resident population is approximately 432,000.  This figure can rise due to commuting into the functional urban area to approximately 477,000

Cagliari | Cagliari ( , or ; ] ; Sardinian: "Casteddu" ; ) is an Italian municipality and the capital of the island of Sardinia, an autonomous region of Italy.  Cagliari's Sardinian name "Casteddu" literally means "castle".  It has about 150,000 inhabitants, while its metropolitan city (including Cagliari and 16 other nearby municipalities) has more than 431,000 inhabitants.  According to Eurostat, the population of the Functional urban area, the commuting zone of Cagliari, rises to 476,974.

Pula, Sardinia | Pula (Latin: Nora) is a "comune" (municipality) in the Metropolitan City of Cagliari in the Italian region of Sardinia, located about 25 km southwest of Cagliari.  As of November 30, 2015, it had a population of 7.422, and an area of 138.7 km2 .

Settimo San Pietro | Settimo San Pietro, Sètimu in Sardinian language, is a "comune" (municipality) of the Metropolitan City of Cagliari in the Italian region Sardinia, located about 9 km northeast of Cagliari, it had a population of 6,532 (2011 census) and an area of 23.2 km2 .

Maracalagonis | Maracalagonis, Mara-Calagonis in sardinian language (mara = marsh), is a "comune" (municipality) in the Metropolitan City of Cagliari in the Italian region Sardinia, located about 12 km northeast of Cagliari.  As of 31 December 2004, it had a population of 6,961 and an area of 101.5 km2 .

Monastir, Sardinia | Monastir (Sardinian: "Muristenis" ) is a "comune" (municipality) of the Cagliari metropolitan area in the Province of South Sardinia in the Italian region of Sardinia, located about 20 km northwest of Cagliari.  As of 2011 census, it had a population of 4,505 inhabitants and an area of 31.8 km2 .

Quartu Sant'Elena | Quartu Sant'Elena ] (Sardinian: "Cuartu Sant'Aleni" ), located four miles East from Cagliari on the ancient Roman road, is a city and "comune" in the Metropolitan City of Cagliari, Sardinia, Italy.  It is the third biggest city of Sardinia with a population of 71,216 as of 2015 .
response.summary_2: The municipality is Cagliari. Cagliari has about 150,000 inhabitants; its metropolitan city has roughly 431,000–432,000 residents, while the functional urban area rises to about 477,000.
